originalTitle: Q2 Earnings Season Nears Kickoff: Bank Earnings in Focus
summary: The overall revisions trend heading into the start of the Q2 cycle remains positive, with expectations steadily increasing over recent months. JPMorgan, Bank of America, Citigroup, and Wells Fargo will help start the June-quarter reporting cycle for the Finance sector on July 14th.
content:
Note: The following is an excerpt from this week's Earnings Trends report. You can access the full report that contains detailed historical actual and estimates for the current and following periods, please click here>>>

The overall earnings picture remains strong and broad-based. We saw that in the last earnings season, when companies not only comfortably beat consensus estimates but also provided reassuring reads on macro and business trends despite elevated energy costs and other risks. All of this has helped sustain a favorable revisions trend, with estimates for the current and upcoming quarters rising.

Total Q2 earnings for the S&P 500 index are currently expected to be up +23.9% from the same period last year on +11.7% higher revenues, with 11 of the 16 Zacks sectors expected to enjoy positive earnings growth.

Q2 earnings estimates have been steadily going up since the quarter got underway, with the current +23.9% growth rate up from +18% at the start of April. Estimates have increased for 5 of the 16 Zacks sectors, including Tech, Energy, Basic Materials, Utilities, and Business Services.

Excluding the significant upward revisions to Energy sector estimates, aggregate Q2 earnings estimates for the remainder of the S&P 500 index would still be in positive territory since the start of April.

Bank Earnings in Focus as Q2 Earnings Season Takes the Spotlight

JPMorgan JPM, Bank of America BAC, Citigroup C and Wells Fargo WFC kick off the June-quarter reporting cycle for the Finance sector on July 14th. Bank stocks in general and these four stocks in particular have enjoyed a decent but otherwise unspectacular run this year, as some of the earlier geopolitical risk factors have eased lately. Banks are cyclical businesses, so any real or perceived reduction in economic risk is positive for their outlook.

The chart below shows the year-to-date performance of JPMorgan, Bank of America, Citigroup and Wells Fargo shares relative to the S&P 500 index and the Zacks Finance sector.

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

The revisions trend is positive as a whole, with Q2 estimates for JPMorgan, Bank of America, and Citigroup modestly moving higher, while the same for Wells Fargo are going down a bit.

JPMorgan is expected to earn $5.49 per share on $48.7 billion in revenues in Q2, representing year-over-year changes of +10.7% and +8.5%, respectively. The Zacks Consensus EPS estimate for JPMorgan has increased +1.9% over the past month and +3% over the last three months. Q2 estimates for Bank of America and Citigroup have increased +2.8% and +4.7% over the last three months, while the same for Wells Fargo have decreased by -1.1%.

Total Q2 earnings for the Zacks Investment Banks/Managers industry, of which JPMorgan, Bank of America, Citigroup and Wells Fargo are a part, are expected to increase by +10.4% from the same period last year on +10.7% higher revenues, as the table below shows.

The growth in Q2 will be coming from the core banking and trading franchises, with investment banking activities largely stable. On the core banking side, loan growth is expected to accelerate further from the very strong numbers in the preceding quarter. For context, loan growth has trended below historical averages over the past three years, but the growth pace began improving in 2025, and the trend continued into 2026 Q1. The favorable outlook for loan portfolios bodes well for net interest income in Q2 and beyond, even though the yield curve lost some of its steepness in Q2.

On the investment banking front, we should get solid numbers from the capital markets side of the business, particularly on the equity capital markets front. But M&A activities have been underwhelming, reflecting the effects of geopolitical uncertainties. Trading revenues remained robust in Q2, with mid-quarter updates indicating growth rates in the +10% to +15% range.

Aggregate trends on the credit quality front have been benign, as reflected in household and commercial delinquencies, bankruptcies, debt-service and other metrics. But the market's focus will be private-credit exposure for banks, as the space has been in the spotlight lately for its exposure to the software and data-center industries.

For the Finance sector as a whole, Q2 earnings are expected to increase by +12.5% on +8.1% higher revenues, which will follow the sector's +25.6% earnings growth on +9.8% higher revenues in the preceding period. The chart below shows the earnings and revenue growth picture for the Zacks Finance sector on a quarterly basis.

The chart below shows the sector's earnings growth picture on an annual basis.

The Finance sector is the second largest earnings contributor to the S&P 500 index, behind only the Tech sector, accounting for 16.4% of the index's expected forward 12-month earnings.

Keeping Track of the Revisions Trend

The expected decline in Energy sector estimates notwithstanding, the overall revisions trend continues to be positive, with estimates for 2026 Q2 and full-year 2026 increasing. This favorable earnings backdrop is evident in the revisions trend, as seen in how expectations for 2026 Q2 have evolved in recent weeks.

The sectors enjoying positive estimate revisions since the start of April include Energy, Tech, Basic Materials, Utilities, and Business Services. Aggregate Q2 earnings estimates would still be positive since the start of the period, even without favorable revisions for the Energy sector, but aggregate estimates would be down if we exclude the increases in the Energy and Tech sector estimates.

The Tech sector has been enjoying positive estimate revisions for more than a year now, so the sector's ongoing positive revisions trend is basically more of the same. We have discussed in this space the positive revisions that the Mag 7 group has been experiencing.

On the negative side, Q2 estimates have come under renewed pressure since the start of the period for the Transportation, Autos, Me
chineseTitle: 第二季度财报季即将拉开帷幕：银行盈利成为焦点
chineseSummary: 第二季度周期开始时的总体修正趋势仍然积极，近几个月预期稳步上升。摩根大通、美国银行、花旗集团和富国银行将于 7 月 14 日帮助启动金融业的六月季度报告周期。
chineseContent:
注意：以下是本周收入趋势报告的摘录。您可以访问完整报告，其中包含当前和后续期间的详细历史实际值和估计值，请点击此处>>>

整体盈利前景依然强劲且基础广泛。我们在上个财报季看到了这一点，尽管能源成本和其他风险上升，但企业不仅轻松超出了普遍预期，而且还提供了令人放心的宏观和商业趋势解读。所有这些都有助于维持有利的修正趋势，当前和未来几个季度的预期不断上升。

目前，标普 500 指数第二季度总收益预计将比去年同期增长 23.9%，收入增长 11.7%，16 个 Zacks 行业中的 11 个行业预计将实现正收益增长。

自第二季度开始以来，第二季度盈利预期一直在稳步上升，目前的增长率为 23.9%，高于 4 月初的 18%。 16 个 Zacks 行业中的 5 个行业的预估有所增加，包括科技、能源、基础材料、公用事业和商业服务。

排除能源行业预期的大幅上调，自 4 月初以来，标准普尔 500 指数其余部分的第二季度总体盈利预期仍将处于正值区域。

第二季度财报季成为焦点，银行盈利成为焦点

摩根大通 (JPMorgan)、美国银行 (Bank of America BAC)、花旗集团 (Citigroup C) 和富国银行 (Wells Fargo) WFC 于 7 月 14 日启动金融业 6 月季度报告周期。总体而言，银行股，特别是这四只股票，今年表现不错，但在其他方面并不引人注目，因为一些早期的地缘政治风险因素最近有所缓解。银行是周期性企业，因此任何实际或感知的经济风险降低都对其前景有利。

下图显示了摩根大通、美国银行、花旗集团和富国银行股票相对于标准普尔 500 指数和 Zacks 金融板块的年初至今表现。

图片来源：Zacks 投资研究

总体而言，修正趋势是积极的，摩根大通、美国银行和花旗集团第二季度的预期小幅上调，而富国银行的第二季度预期则略有下降。

摩根大通预计第二季度每股收益为 5.49 美元，收入为 487 亿美元，同比分别增长 +10.7% 和 +8.5%。 Zacks 对摩根大通的共识每股收益估计在过去一个月增加了 1.9%，在过去三个月增加了 3%。过去三个月，美国银行和花旗集团第二季度的预期分别增加了+2.8%和+4.7%，而富国银行的第二季度预期则下降了-1.1%。

Zacks 投资银行/管理公司行业（摩根大通、美国银行、花旗集团和富国银行等）第二季度总收益预计将比去年同期增长 10.4%，收入增长 10.7%，如下表所示。

第二季度的增长将来自核心银行和交易业务，投资银行业务基本稳定。在核心银行业务方面，贷款增长预计将比上一季度的强劲数字进一步加速。就背景而言，过去三年贷款增长趋势低于历史平均水平，但增长速度在 2025 年开始改善，并且这一趋势持续到 2026 年第一季度。尽管收益率曲线在第二季度失去了一些陡峭程度，但贷款组合的良好前景对第二季度及以后的净利息收入来说是个好兆头。

在投资银行方面，我们应该从业务的资本市场方面获得可靠的数据，特别是在股权资本市场方面。但并购活动一直平淡无奇，反映出地缘政治不确定性的影响。第二季度交易收入依然强劲，季度中期更新显示增长率在 +10% 至 +15% 范围内。

信贷质量方面的总体趋势一直是良性的，这反映在家庭和商业拖欠、破产、偿债和其他指标上。但市场的焦点将是银行的私人信贷风险，因为该领域最近因其对软件和数据中心行业的风险而成为人们关注的焦点。

对于整个金融行业来说，第二季度收益预计将增长 12.5%，收入增加 8.1%，此前该行业的收益增长 25.6%，收入增加 9.8%。下图显示了 Zacks Finance 部门的季度收益和收入增长情况。

下图显示了该行业的年度盈利增长情况。

金融板块是标准普尔 500 指数第二大盈利贡献者，仅次于科技板块，占该指数未来 12 个月预期盈利的 16.4%。

跟踪修订趋势

尽管能源行业预测预计会下降，但总体修正趋势仍然积极，2026 年第二季度和 2026 年全年的预测均有所增加。这种有利的盈利背景在修正趋势中显而易见，从最近几周对 2026 年第二季度的预期的演变即可看出。

自 4 月初以来，预测值出现积极修正的行业包括能源、科技、基础材料、公用事业和商业服务。自本期初以来，即使能源行业没有出现有利的修正，第二季度总体盈利预期仍将为正值，但如果我们排除能源和科技行业预期的增长，总体预期将会下降。

科技行业一年多来一直在享受积极的预测修正，因此该行业持续的积极修正趋势基本上是相同的。我们在这个空间中讨论了 Mag 7 组正在经历的积极修改。

不利的一面是，自本季度开始以来，交通、汽车、交通运输业第二季度的预测再次面临压力。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
